findings.title: update_nvr docstring explains WHAT and contains speculative forward-looking language
findings.rule: Global CLAUDE.md, Comments policy: 'Default to no comments. Only add when the WHY is non-obvious... Don't explain WHAT the code does... Don't reference the current task, fix, or callers... those belong in the PR description and rot as the codebase evolves.'
findings.violation: The added docstring has two problems. First, 'Targets the integration-API ``nvrs`` resource for symmetry with :meth:`get_nvr`' describes what the code does (calls `nvrs`), which is already obvious from the implementation. Second, 'Untested against live hardware in readonly CI; if a future deployment exercises this in readwrite mode and the integration API requires a different verb or path, surface the failure here' is a speculative future scenario referencing current CI state — this will rot and belongs in the issue tracker, not a docstring.

findings.title: ProtectClient class docstring pins a specific firmware version that will rot
findings.rule: Global CLAUDE.md, Comments policy: 'Don't reference the current task, fix, or callers ("used by X", "added for the Y flow", "handles the case from issue #123") — those belong in the PR description and rot as the codebase evolves.'
findings.violation: The class docstring says 'verified against UCK-G2-Plus running Protect ``7.0.107``'. A firmware version reference embedded in source code will become stale on the first hardware upgrade and mislead readers. The verification context belongs in the PR description, not the class docstring.

findings.title: PR/commit subject line exceeds 72-character limit
findings.rule: Global CLAUDE.md, Commits: 'Imperative mood, <=72 char subject line, one logical change per commit.'
findings.violation: The PR title 'fix: switch ProtectClient to /proxy/protect/integration/v1/ X-API-Key path (#103)' is 81 characters, exceeding the 72-character limit by 9 characters.

findings.title: get_nvr docstring body explains WHAT rather than a non-obvious WHY
findings.rule: Global CLAUDE.md, Comments policy: 'Only add when the WHY is non-obvious — a hidden constraint, a subtle invariant, a workaround for a specific bug, behavior that would surprise a reader.'
findings.violation: The added docstring body 'The integration API exposes the NVR at ``nvrs`` (plural) and returns a single object — there is exactly one NVR per controller' describes the API behavior (WHAT it returns). The plural-endpoint-returns-single-object behavior is mildly surprising, which gives this moderate justification; however the one-NVR-per-controller fact is domain knowledge rather than a hidden code constraint. Confidence is moderate because a reader encountering a plural endpoint that returns a scalar could reasonably be surprised.

findings.title: Module docstring still describes the removed xfail marker as present
findings.rule: Global CLAUDE.md, Comments policy: 'Don't reference the current task, fix, or callers... those belong in the PR description and rot as the codebase evolves.'
findings.violation: The module docstring says 'The Protect test is marked xfail(strict=True) while #103 is open: it proves the bug exists today, and the moment #103 ships a fix the marker forces the test to flip...' The PR removes the xfail marker but leaves this description intact, making it immediately inaccurate. The diff touches this file (removes `import pytest`) so the PR author had the opportunity to clean up the docstring.

residual_risks.description: update_nvr is explicitly noted as 'untested against live hardware in readonly CI' in the docstring. The PUT path to `nvrs` is inferred by symmetry with GET `nvrs`, not verified. If the integration API uses a different path or verb for NVR updates, this will silently fail at runtime in readwrite mode with no unit-test coverage to catch it.

test_list_events_returns_list in tests/integration/test_protect_live.py is marked @pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True) but the decorator does not include @pytest.mark.asyncio (or equivalent), which the other integration tests appear to rely on via a pytest-asyncio mode setting. If the asyncio mode is not 'auto', the test may be collected as a synchronous coroutine and never actually execute the await, causing xfail to pass for the wrong reason.

testing_gaps.description: No unit test exercises the code path where get_nvr or get_bootstrap returns HTTP 404 and the tool layer surfaces a ToolError with an agent-readable message. The PR documents that these endpoints 404 in production but only adds an integration xfail; there is no unit test asserting that the 404 is translated through the error-mapping chain correctly.
